summary: EU foreign policy chief Kallas accused Russia at G7 of 'helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans' and supplying drones. UK intelligence confirmed Russian assistance. Rubio said Russia denied it and US takes them at their word — but did not deny the underlying claim.

actions.text: Called emergency UN Security Council session, blamed US escalation
Delivered S-400 components to Iran via Caspian Sea route
Vetoed UN Security Council ceasefire resolution
Increased oil exports to offset global supply disruption
Reportedly sharing satellite intelligence with Iranian forces
Reportedly supplying Iran with replacement air defense components via overland route through Turkmenistan
Putin calls Mojtaba Khamenei to congratulate new Supreme Leader; reaffirms support
Trump and Putin discuss Iran war and Ukraine peace prospects. Putin warns of global energy crisis.
Russia contacts Iran regarding ceasefire alongside China and France. Putin writes to Mojtaba Khamenei reiterating Moscow's support.
European intelligence sources report Russia sharing satellite imagery and electronic intelligence with Iran targeting US carrier positions
Russia condemns IEA's 400M barrel emergency release as 'weaponization of energy reserves.' Proposes BRICS alternative energy framework.
Trump-Putin phone call discussed potential sanctions waivers for Russian oil exports to offset Hormuz disruption. Kremlin confirms call but denies formal agreement reached.
Russian consulate in Isfahan closes due to ongoing US-Israeli strikes in the area. Moscow cites safety concerns for diplomatic staff amid deadly weekend attacks.
Iran FM Araghchi confirmed Russia providing 'military cooperation' as a 'strategic partner.' Closed Isfahan consulate citing security concerns.
WSJ reports Russia sharing satellite imagery of US force locations and improved Shahed drone components with Iran, drawing on Ukraine experience.
Russia condemns IDF strike on Bandar Anzali naval base on Caspian Sea — threatens the Russia-Iran supply corridor.
Russia abstains on UNSC Resolution 2817. Introduced counter-resolution that was not adopted. Bandar Anzali strike disrupts Iran-Russia weapons transit route.
Providing Iran with intelligence including precise locations of US warships and aircraft (WaPo report).
Russian Foreign Ministry condemned Israel's Nowruz strikes on Tehran as 'provocative targeting of civilian celebrations during a sacred cultural holiday.' Russia abstained on UNSC Resolution 2817.
Russian FM Zakharova formally protested Israel's bombing of Bandar Anzali on the Caspian Sea — a key port used to transit goods between Iran and Russia. Called for ceasefire.

Putin told Iran Moscow remains 'a loyal friend and reliable partner' during this difficult period. Russia condemned the US-Israeli strike on Natanz as 'a blatant violation of international law' and called for an immediate end to hostilities.

Russia condemned Natanz nuclear facility strike as 'a blatant violation of international law.' Putin pledged Russia remains 'loyal friend to Iran.' Iranian FM Araghchi confirmed Russia is helping Iran 'in many different directions' including intelligence support for drone strikes.

EU foreign policy chief Kallas publicly discloses at G7 that Russia is actively providing Iran with intelligence to target and kill American forces, confirmed by UK British intelligence. US envoy Witkoff contradicts allies saying Russia denied it. France confirms Russia-Iran intel cooperation "has existed and continues."

G7 foreign ministers meeting (Vaux-de-Cernay, France): EU chief Kallas publicly disclosed Russia is providing satellite imagery to Iran, sharing locations of US warships and aircraft, upgrading Iranian drones, and helping Iran target American forces. UK Defense Secretary Healey confirmed via British intelligence. France FM Barrot: credible evidence Russia's support is directed particularly at American targets. US envoy Witkoff downplayed claim, taking Russia's denial at face value.
Pezeshkian called Putin to reaffirm Iran-Russia coordination and discuss Islamabad peace talks framework.

statements.speaker: Foreign Minister Lavrov
statements.text: Russia condemns the US military escalation and calls for immediate ceasefire.

statements.speaker: Kremlin Spokesperson
statements.text: Any NATO involvement in the region will be met with appropriate response.

statements.speaker: President Putin
statements.text: The cynical violation of all norms of human morality and international law. A global energy crisis threatens the world economy.

statements.speaker: Foreign Ministry
statements.text: The IEA reserve release is a political act designed to mask the catastrophic consequences of American aggression.

statements.speaker: Amb. Nebenzia (UNSC)
statements.text: If someone who is not well versed in international affairs reads this [UNSC 2817], then this person will be left with the impression that Tehran, on its own volition and out of malice, conducted an unprovoked attack on Arab states.

statements.speaker: Putin
statements.text: Congratulated Mojtaba Khamenei on appointment as Supreme Leader.

statements.speaker: President Vladimir Putin
statements.text: Moscow remains a loyal friend and reliable partner to Iran during this difficult period. The strike on Natanz is a blatant violation of international law.

statements.speaker: Kaja Kallas (EU) at G7
statements.text: Russia is helping Iran with intelligence to target Americans, to kill Americans.

summary: China maintains strategic ambiguity — formally opposes the war, facilitates back-channel messages, but COSCO ships blocked at Hormuz despite Iran's claimed exemption, revealing limits of Sino-Iranian solidarity. China is Iran's largest oil customer (1.5M bbl/day), facing economic pressure from Hormuz closure.

actions.text: Called for restraint from all parties at UN General Assembly
Continued purchasing Iranian oil despite US sanctions
Proposed 4-point peace plan at UN Security Council
Chinese-made drone components identified in Iranian Shahed drones
Began stockpiling strategic petroleum reserves amid price surge
Chinese oil tankers spotted loading Iranian crude at Bandar Abbas despite Western sanctions
Chinese Foreign Minister warns US that ground invasion of Iran would be 'crossing a red line'
Barred export of rare earth elements for military use — could impact US weapons replenishment during conflict
Contacted Iran regarding ceasefire along with Russia and France. Xi prioritizing US relations over Iran; summit with Trump planned in Beijing
China increases purchases of Iranian crude at steep discounts as Western buyers abandon the market. Beijing stockpiling reserves amid IEA 400M barrel release.
Trump claims China will send warships to help reopen Strait of Hormuz. China has not confirmed participation in any naval coalition.
Iran FM Araghchi confirmed China providing 'military cooperation' as a 'strategic partner.' Chinese-linked vessels among few still transiting Hormuz.
Iran sends 11.7M barrels of oil to China via shadow fleet. Trump delays China summit to focus on war. Chinese-linked vessels among few still transiting Hormuz.
Trump delays China trip by a month, saying 'We've got a war going on.' Shadow fleet continues receiving Iranian oil via Hormuz.
China abstains on UNSC Resolution 2817 condemning Iran's Gulf attacks. Chinese FM: 'This is a war that should not have happened, and a war that benefits no one.'
Exported advanced radar systems; transitioned Iran to BeiDou-3 navigation. Iran nearing deal for 50 CM-302 supersonic antiship missiles from China.
Barred export of rare earth elements for military use. Chinese buyers purchasing 90%+ of Iranian oil at discount.

China called for 'immediate cessation' of Nowruz strikes on Tehran. Chinese FM said Iran's sacred holiday should be respected. China's tankers remain the primary buyers of Iranian crude — US unsanctioning plan targets redirecting this oil away from China.
China called for an immediate ceasefire and condemned military operations targeting civilian infrastructure. China's ships remain among the nations granted Hormuz transit rights by Iran.
FM Wang Yi calls Araghchi, urges all sides to "seize opportunities for dialogue" and pursue early peace talks. China supports Pakistan as mediator.

Two COSCO container vessels (CSCL Indian Ocean, CSCL Arctic Ocean) turned back by IRGC Navy near Larak Island while attempting to transit the Strait of Hormuz — despite Iran's stated exemption for Chinese ships. COSCO had announced March 25 it was restarting Middle East bookings. China had not publicly commented on the incident.
FM Wang Yi calls Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar to praise Pakistan's mediation efforts at Islamabad summit. China reiterates call for ceasefire and urges all parties to resume dialogue.

statements.speaker: Foreign Ministry
statements.text: China calls on all parties to exercise maximum restraint and return to diplomacy.

statements.speaker: Ambassador to UN
statements.text: The international community must prioritize civilian protection and humanitarian access.

statements.speaker: Wang Yi
statements.text: The attack came as negotiations made significant progress. China calls for immediate cessation of military operations.

statements.speaker: Foreign Ministry
statements.text: Hengaw's report of 4,300+ killed underscores the urgent need for immediate ceasefire. The humanitarian catastrophe demands international action.

statements.speaker: China FM (UNSC)
statements.text: This is a war that should not have happened, and a war that benefits no one.

summary: EU split between G7 members (France, Germany, Italy) attending Paris talks and neutral members. Kallas confirmed at G7 that Russia is supplying Iran with intelligence to target Americans. G7 issued joint statement demanding permanent Hormuz reopening and civilian protection. Germany's FM Wadephul confirmed direct US-Iran meeting 'prepared' in Pakistan.

actions.text: Emergency EU foreign ministers meeting called within hours of first strikes
Expanded sanctions on Iranian Revolutionary Guard leadership
EU High Representative began shuttle diplomacy between Tehran and Washington
Pledged €500M emergency humanitarian aid package for affected civilians
Activated strategic oil reserves to stabilize European energy markets
EU Parliament passes non-binding resolution calling for immediate ceasefire by 482-67 vote
EU High Representative condemns Hezbollah attack on Cyprus as 'attack on Europe'
Von der Leyen calls to 'bring Europe's mutual defence clause to life.' EUAA preparing for potential refugee influx from Iran's 90 million population
European natural gas prices up ~40% from Hormuz disruption. European allies stressed they did NOT participate in strikes
EU welcomes IEA 400M barrel reserve release. European Commission warns of cascading food price crisis if Hormuz remains closed through spring planting season.
IEA authorizes record 400M barrel reserve release. EU members contributing significant portion.
Emergency session of all 27 EU foreign ministers convened. No European leader argued the war is lawful. Joint statement calls for immediate ceasefire and humanitarian access.
EU High Representative Kallas proposes European naval mission to help reopen Strait of Hormuz under EU command, separate from US-led coalition. Proposal faces divisions among member states.
EU foreign ministers reject expanding Aspides naval mission to Hormuz. Kallas proposes 'Black Sea model' oil corridor with UN. Germany's Pistorius: 'This is not our war.'
EU High Representative Kallas condemns Larijani killing as 'dangerous escalation.' Reiterates call for immediate ceasefire and humanitarian access.
Trump threatens to leave NATO after EU member states refuse to join Hormuz escort coalition. Kallas says 'no appetite' among members.

Kallas calls on US and Israel to end war in Reuters exclusive. EU consulting Gulf states, Jordan, Egypt on face-saving diplomatic exit. Discusses Black Sea grain deal-style Hormuz corridor with UN Secretary-General Guterres.
Kallas actively consulting Gulf states on face-saving ceasefire proposal for Iran, Israel, and US
EU summit in Brussels refuses to join US military campaign or help secure Strait of Hormuz. Spanish PM: 'This war is illegal.'
Six Western allies (UK, France, Germany, Japan +2) issue joint statement ready to ensure safe passage through Hormuz. UK sends military planners to CENTCOM.

EU Foreign Ministers reject expanding Aspides naval mission to Hormuz. Kallas: 'Member states do not have an appetite to go to this war.' But UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands separately join 7-nation Hormuz coalition statement.
European Council: moratorium on energy/water strikes, reopen Hormuz. 6-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, FR, DE, IT, NL, JP). ECB: 'material impact on near-term inflation.'

EU foreign policy chief Kallas condemned Nowruz strikes, saying attacks on civilian celebrations are 'deeply disturbing.' EU maintains ceasefire call and moratorium demand on civilian infrastructure strikes.

EU Member States approved new sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations. EU High Representative Kallas held calls with Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea to coordinate on Hormuz.

EU approved new sanctions against 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations. HR/VP Kallas held calls with Turkey, Qatar, South Korea on Hormuz reopening and conflict de-escalation. EU declined to send NATO warships to Hormuz.

EU foreign policy chief Kallas held calls with Iran FM Araghchi and counterparts in Turkey, Qatar, and South Korea on conflict, energy infrastructure strikes, and Hormuz pressure. 22 EU-linked nations joining NATO Hormuz coalition.

EU formally declined military involvement in Hormuz operations. Kallas: "Nobody wants to go actively in this war." 22-nation coalition forming for freedom of navigation monitoring. ECB postponed rate cuts; raised 2026 inflation forecast as European gas prices remain near double pre-war levels.
EU approved new sanctions targeting 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations per Kallas announcement.

G7 joint statement: calls for 'absolute necessity to permanently restore safe and toll-free freedom of navigation in Strait of Hormuz' and 'immediate cessation of attacks against civilians.' Rubio sparred with European allies over war strategy.

EU foreign policy chief Kallas stated at G7 France meeting that Russia is providing Iran with intelligence 'to kill Americans' and supplying drone technology. G7 Foreign Ministers issued joint statement calling for civilian protection and Hormuz reopening. 35 countries joined France-hosted Hormuz military conference.
Kallas reported G7 had 'no appetite' for military Hormuz mission while war continues; G7 joint statement conditions reopening on ceasefire.
EU ambassadors approve new targeted sanctions on 19 Iranian regime officials and entities for human rights violations. Kallas X post confirms EU commitment to holding Iran accountable.

statements.speaker: EU High Representative
statements.text: Europe stands ready to mediate. There is no military solution to this conflict.

statements.speaker: European Council President
statements.text: We must prevent this conflict from spreading further across the region.

statements.speaker: EU High Representative
statements.text: The Hengaw death toll of 4,300 demands immediate international investigation and ceasefire.

statements.speaker: Kaja Kallas
statements.text: We have been consulting with Gulf countries about proposals so everybody saves face. The problem with wars is it's easier to start than to stop.

statements.speaker: Kaja Kallas, EU Foreign Policy Chief
statements.text: There is no appetite in Europe to expand our naval presence or join this military campaign.

statements.speaker: HR/VP Kaja Kallas
statements.text: The EU continues to hold Iran accountable. Today, EU Member States approved new sanctions targeting 19 regime officials. We stand firm in calling for an immediate ceasefire and opening of the Strait of Hormuz.

statements.speaker: G7 Joint Statement
statements.text: The G7 underlines the absolute necessity to permanently restore safe and toll-free freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

summary: First military casualty on Day 13 — French soldier killed in Iraqi militia drone attack on counterterrorism base. Macron called death 'unacceptable.' Carrier group deployed to Eastern Mediterranean. Escalating from observer to directly affected party.

actions.text: Deployed Charles de Gaulle carrier group to Eastern Mediterranean
Began evacuating French nationals from Lebanon and Iraq
Hosted back-channel talks between Iranian and Israeli diplomats in Paris
Suspended all arms exports to the region pending conflict resolution
Macron places French forces on heightened alert after Hezbollah drone hits Cyprus
Macron: 'An attack on Cyprus is an attack on all of Europe' — invokes EU mutual defense clause
French soldier killed in Iraqi militia drone attack on counterterrorism base — first French military fatality of the conflict
Macron calls death 'unacceptable,' demands full investigation. Stresses France in Iraq for counterterrorism only.
Trump claims France will send warships to help reopen Strait of Hormuz. France has not officially confirmed.
EU continues diplomatic pressure for ceasefire. Treasury Secretary Bessent met Chinese VP He Lifeng in Paris on economic stabilization.
France joins G5 statement (with Canada, Germany, Italy, UK) warning Israel against 'significant ground offensive' in Lebanon.
France joins G5 statement warning Israel against expanded Lebanon ground offensive. Macron calls for restraint after Larijani assassination.
Joined 5-nation statement (Canada, France, Germany, Italy, UK) calling Lebanon ground invasion to be averted.
Macron explicitly rules out French participation in any Hormuz unblocking operations: 'France would never take part in the current context.'
Macron calls Trump and Qatar Emir urging moratorium on civilian infrastructure strikes. France at UNSC calls Iran 'primarily responsible' for escalation. Voted for Resolution 2817.

France signs 7-nation Hormuz joint statement calling for safe passage and infrastructure moratorium. Macron separately urges moratorium on civilian infrastructure strikes in calls with Trump and Qatar Emir.
European Council: moratorium on energy/water strikes, reopen Hormuz. 6-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, FR, DE, IT, NL, JP). ECB: 'material impact on near-term inflation.'

statements.speaker: President
statements.text: France will protect its citizens and work tirelessly for peace in the region.

statements.speaker: President Macron
statements.text: The death of a French soldier in Iraq is unacceptable. France is present in Iraq solely for counterterrorism. We demand accountability.

statements.speaker: President Macron
statements.text: No one can tell what Trump wants from this ongoing war with Iran. (After G7 video call)

statements.speaker: President Macron
statements.text: France would never take part in operations to unblock the Strait of Hormuz in the current context.

summary: Germany's FM Wadephul confirmed on-record that a direct US-Iran meeting has been 'prepared' and is expected 'very soon in Pakistan' — the most specific diplomatic confirmation of impending direct talks since the war began. Merz has called bombing Iran counterproductive for regime change.

actions.text: Immediately suspended all arms exports to Middle East conflict parties
Deployed Bundeswehr medical teams to Jordan for refugee assistance
Chancellor spoke with US President urging de-escalation
Allocated €200M for energy market stabilization measures
Chancellor condemns 'spiral of violence' and Swiss declaration as 'brave and necessary'
Germany announces emergency €1B energy stabilization fund as gas prices surge
Government spokesperson declared war 'has nothing to do with NATO' and said German participation in Hormuz patrols 'is not being considered.'
Defense Minister Pistorius reiterates 'This is not our war' after Larijani killing. Germany opposes any European military involvement in Hormuz.
Confirmed refusal to join US Hormuz escort coalition. Joined 5-nation statement opposing Lebanon ground invasion.
Merz shifts to open criticism: bombing Iran 'futile' for regime change, warns of perpetual war, refuses Hormuz involvement
Germany joins six-nation Hormuz safe passage statement. European natural gas prices up 35% on Gulf energy attacks.
Germany signs 7-nation Hormuz joint statement despite FM Wadephul earlier saying Berlin had 'no intention' of joining military operations during conflict.
European Council: moratorium on energy/water strikes, reopen Hormuz. 6-nation Hormuz coalition (UK, FR, DE, IT, NL, JP). ECB: 'material impact on near-term inflation.'
Germany explicitly ruled out military participation in any Hormuz enforcement operation. Joined UAE, Bahrain, UK, France joint statement condemning Iran's de facto Hormuz closure.

Defense Minister Boris Pistorius at G7 in France: 'To make it crystal clear, this war is a catastrophe for the world's economies. European partners and Germany highlighted from the beginning that we have not been consulted before.' Germany reiterated no military participation.

FM Wadephul confirmed on Deutschlandfunk radio that US-Iran indirect contacts have taken place and a direct meeting is 'prepared' and expected 'very soon in Pakistan.' Called it 'first signs of hope and trust.' Also confirmed Russia providing satellite intelligence to Iran and Germany lobbied US to pressure Moscow.
FM Johann Wadephul confirms direct US-Iran meeting in Pakistan is "prepared for very soon." Germany urges Iran to engage in good faith through the Islamabad track.

statements.speaker: Chancellor
statements.text: Germany stands firmly on the side of international law and civilian protection.

statements.speaker: Chancellor Friedrich Merz
statements.text: NATO is a defensive alliance, not an offensive one. We will not participate in reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

statements.speaker: FM Johann Wadephul
statements.text: According to my information, there have been indirect contacts. And they have also prepared for a direct meeting expected very soon in Pakistan. Initial positions have already been exchanged in writing.

summary: First traditionally neutral Western nation to formally condemn US-Israel strikes as violating international law.

actions.text: Defense Minister Pfister declares US-Israel attacks violate international law
Cardinal Parolin (Vatican SoS) called for end to war, rejected 'preventive war' justification.

statements.speaker: Defense Minister Pfister
statements.text: These attacks constitute clear violations of international humanitarian law. Switzerland cannot remain silent.

summary: NATO member directly involved after Iranian ballistic missile entered Turkish airspace and was intercepted by NATO Patriot batteries. Turkey invoked Article 4 NATO consultations.

actions.text: Placed forces on heightened alert after Iranian drone strike on Nakhchivan near Turkish border
NATO Patriot battery in southern Turkey intercepted Iranian ballistic missile that entered Turkish airspace
Turkey condemned Iranian airspace violation as a hostile act; invoked NATO Article 4 consultations
Turkish Foreign Ministry summoned Iranian ambassador to protest repeated airspace violations
Turkey offers to mediate ceasefire. Erdogan calls for 'humanitarian corridor' through Turkish-Iranian border for aid and refugees.
NATO air defenses intercept 3rd Iranian ballistic missile over Turkey near Adana at 3:25 AM local time. Third intercept over NATO territory since war began.

Foreign Minister Fidan held calls with Iranian FM Araghchi, Egyptian FM, EU Foreign Policy Chief Kallas, and US officials on March 22. Fidan warned of "growing assessment that US and Israel initial positions are drifting apart" risking a longer war. Turkey positioning as key ceasefire mediator.

statements.speaker: Turkish Ministry of Defense
statements.text: Any violation of Turkish airspace will be met with the full force of NATO's collective defense capabilities.

statements.speaker: Turkish Foreign Minister
statements.text: Iran must immediately cease all missile launches that endanger Turkish sovereignty and civilian safety.

statements.speaker: President Erdogan
statements.text: Turkey is ready to facilitate dialogue. The Hengaw reports of 4,300+ dead demand immediate ceasefire.

statements.speaker: FM Hakan Fidan
statements.text: Israel is moving toward a new genocide in Lebanon. The international community must act before it is too late.

statements.speaker: Turkish MFA
statements.text: Turkey condemns the expansion of Israeli military operations into Lebanon. This aggressive action threatens regional stability.

statements.speaker: FM Hakan Fidan
statements.text: Turkey is making every effort for peace and, primarily, a ceasefire. The initial positions of the US and Israel appear to be drifting apart, which could prolong the war.

summary: Lebanon has been drawn into the conflict as Hezbollah launched rockets at Israel from Lebanese territory, triggering devastating Israeli airstrikes. Over 1,000 Lebanese killed, 2,876 injured, and nearly 1 million displaced (20% of the population). On Day 25, Lebanon expelled Iran's ambassador in an unprecedented diplomatic rupture, formally blaming Iran for drawing it into the war.

actions.text: Cabinet voted 22-2 to declare Hezbollah military activities illegal — historic break from decades of tolerance for the militia's armed wing

Lebanon declares Iran's incoming ambassador Mohammad Reza Shibani persona non grata and orders him to leave by March 29. Lebanon also recalls its own ambassador to Tehran and reimposed visa requirements on Iranian nationals. Lebanese FM Raggi cited IRGC's direct command of Hezbollah as a violation of diplomatic norms.

statements.speaker: Lebanese Cabinet
statements.text: All non-state military activities within Lebanese territory are hereby declared unlawful. The state alone holds the monopoly on legitimate force.

summary: Spain permanently withdrew its ambassador to Israel and denied US access to jointly operated military bases in southern Spain for Iran operations. PM Sanchez condemned the war as 'unjustifiable.' Trump threatened trade cuts.

actions.text: Council of Ministers approved decree terminating Ambassador Ana María Sálomon Pérez's appointment to Israel
Denied US access to jointly operated military bases in southern Spain for Iran-related operations
PM Sanchez posted on X demanding Hormuz opening and preservation of all Middle East energy infrastructure, warning of global energy crisis: 'The world should not pay the consequences of this war.'

statements.speaker: PM Pedro Sanchez
statements.text: Our position is clear: no to the war. The US-Israel attack on Iran is unjustifiable.

statements.speaker: FM José Manuel Albares
statements.text: Our embassy in Israel will remain open to assist with all evacuation operations of citizens by all possible means.

summary: Greece imposed 3-month profit margin caps on fuel and food prices to prevent war-driven price gouging. Gas stations limited to 12-cent/liter markup. PM Mitsotakis warned retailers against profiteering.

actions.text: Imposed 3-month profit margin caps on fuel (max 12¢/liter retail markup) and food prices, effective through June 30, 2026

statements.speaker: PM Kyriakos Mitsotakis
statements.text: The government is vigilant for further effects of the conflict. We warn retailers against profiteering.

summary: Pope Leo XIV issued urgent ceasefire demand from St. Peter's Square and escalated with direct phone calls to world leaders. Vatican formally offered to host peace negotiations.

actions.text: Pope Leo XIV delivers public ceasefire demand from St. Peter's Square. Vatican formally offers to host peace talks between all parties.
Pope Leo XIV escalates appeal, personally contacts Trump and Khamenei by phone urging immediate ceasefire
Pope Leo XIV delivers Sunday Angelus appeal: 'Cease the fire!' Vatican confirms direct phone calls to Trump and Mojtaba Khamenei urging immediate ceasefire.
Pope Leo XIV delivered Sunday Angelus ceasefire appeal: 'Persevere in prayer so that hostilities may cease and paths of peace may finally open up.' Trump publicly rejected the appeal hours later.

Pope Leo XIV at Castel Gandolfo renewed ceasefire call: 'I want to renew my call for a ceasefire, to work toward peace, not through weapons, but through dialogue, and to genuinely seek a solution that benefits everyone.' White House: Trump has 'no interest in papal peace counsel.'

statements.speaker: Pope Leo XIV
statements.text: Cease fire! In the name of God, cease fire. I call on all leaders to choose dialogue over destruction.

statements.speaker: Pope Leo XIV
statements.text: Stability and peace are not achieved through mutual threats, nor through the use of weapons, which sow destruction, suffering, and death, but only through reasonable, sincere, and responsible dialogue.

summary: Italy suffered its first hardware loss when an MQ-9 Reaper drone was destroyed at Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait by Iranian attack. First NATO ally to lose military equipment in the conflict.

actions.text: Italian Air Force MQ-9 Reaper drone destroyed at Ali Al Salem airbase, Kuwait — first NATO ally hardware loss in the conflict

statements.speaker: Gen. Luciano Portolano, Chief of Defence
statements.text: I confirm the destruction of an Italian Air Force MQ-9 Reaper at Ali Al Salem airbase. All Italian military personnel are safe.

summary: 11 countries have requested Ukrainian drone expertise and low-cost interceptors. Zelenskyy offers $1K-2K drone interceptors as alternative to $30K systems used against Iranian Shaheds. Ukraine leveraging its battle-tested counter-drone experience.

actions.text: 11 countries request Ukrainian drone expertise; Zelenskyy offers $1K-2K interceptors vs $30K alternatives for countering Iranian Shahed drones

statements.speaker: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy
statements.text: We can provide interceptors at $1,000-2,000 per unit instead of $30,000. Ukraine has more experience fighting Shaheds than anyone.

summary: India faces deepening LPG crisis as Hormuz blockade disrupts energy imports. Only 2 tankers have crossed the strait with Iran's permission. 22 vessels remain stuck. Government considering emergency rationing measures.

actions.text: LPG crisis deepens — only 2 tankers crossed Hormuz with Iran's permission, 22 vessels stuck. Government weighing emergency rationing.

summary: PM Takaichi rules out sending warships to Hormuz coalition despite Trump's pressure. Japan heavily dependent on Gulf oil but maintains pacifist constitution constraints.

actions.text: PM Sanae Takaichi rules out sending Japanese warships to US-led Hormuz reopening coalition, citing constitutional constraints and need for diplomatic solutions.

Iran confirmed Japanese-flagged ships may transit the Strait of Hormuz under Iran's selective blockade policy. Japan had secured permission on March 21 under diplomatic back-channel. Japan continues largest-ever 30-day strategic oil reserve release as part of IEA 400M barrel coordination.

statements.speaker: PM Sanae Takaichi
statements.text: Japan will pursue diplomatic channels to resolve the Strait of Hormuz crisis. Our constitution does not permit the deployment of naval forces to active conflict zones.

summary: UK granted US use of Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford for limited operations after Iran targeted the joint base for the first time. EU sanctions against 19 Iranian officials approved with UK support. UK warships on standby in region.

actions.text: UK joined 7-nation Hormuz safe passage coalition with France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada. Sent military planners to CENTCOM.
PM Starmer authorizes US use of Diego Garcia for strikes on Iranian Hormuz missile sites — drawing UK into active conflict role. Iran FM Araghchi declares UK bases 'legitimate targets.'

UK authorizes US use of Diego Garcia base for Hormuz strike operations. Iran fires two IRBMs at Diego Garcia (both neutralized). Araghchi warns UK: 'PM Starmer is putting British lives in danger.' UK MoD condemns 'Iran's reckless attacks.'

PM Starmer authorized US use of Diego Garcia base for Hormuz missile strikes. Iran immediately fired two IRBMs at Diego Garcia — one intercepted by US Navy SM-3. Araghchi warned UK 'putting British lives in danger.' Saudi Arabia separately expelled Iran's military attaché.

US and Israel Day 23 strikes continue using Diego Garcia base authorized by Starmer. UK faces domestic pressure after Iran fired IRBMs at Diego Garcia in retaliation. Government defends decision as 'necessary to prevent nuclear escalation.'

UK gave US permission to use RAF Fairford and Diego Garcia for 'specific and limited defensive operations' against Iranian missile sites after Iran's first-ever attack on Diego Garcia. UK MoD confirmed Diego Garcia attack and declared it unsuccessful.

statements.speaker: PM Keir Starmer
statements.text: The United Kingdom has authorized the United States to use Diego Garcia for operations targeting Iranian missile sites threatening the Strait of Hormuz.

summary: Saudi Arabia has moved from observer to active military support, granting US access to King Fahd Air Base and reportedly preparing to formally join the coalition. Crown Prince MBS reportedly close to a decision on war entry. Saudi Arabia intercepted 32+ Iranian drones targeting oil infrastructure (Ras Tanura, Ghawar, Abqaiq) on March 25. Saudi Arabia's entry would be the most significant coalitional expansion since the war began.

actions.text: Saudi Arabia quietly granted US basing rights at Prince Sultan Air Base for expanded air operations against Iran, while publicly maintaining 'neutral' status.
Saudi Arabia expelled Iran's military attaché following Iranian attacks targeting Saudi oil infrastructure. Called attack 'blatant aggression.'

Saudi Arabia intercepted 32+ Iranian drones and 1 ballistic missile over 11 hours targeting Ras Tanura and Abqaiq/Ghawar oil facilities — the largest single-day drone wave against Saudi Arabia. Incident marks escalation of Iran directly targeting Saudi energy infrastructure.

Wall Street Journal reports Saudi Arabia and UAE moving closer to formally entering the war on the US-Israel side. Saudi Arabia has already authorized use of military bases for coalition operations, marking a formal break from its previous neutral stance.

Saudi Arabia grants US military access to King Fahd Air Base — reversal of earlier refusal, signaling closer military alignment and potential war entry. Saudi/UAE moving toward formal coalition entry per WSJ, confirmed by Bloomberg and i24.

Saudi Arabia grants US military access to King Fahd Air Base in Dhahran — reversing pre-war policy of refusing US combat operations from Saudi soil. Crown Prince MBS reportedly 'close to a decision' on formally joining the US-Israel coalition against Iran. Saudi Arabia intercepted a drone in the Eastern Province on March 26. FM Faisal bin Farhan: 'Saudi Arabia's patience with Iranian attacks is not unlimited. Any belief that Gulf countries are incapable of responding is a serious miscalculation.'

statements.speaker: Saudi Foreign Ministry
statements.text: The Kingdom calls on all parties to exercise maximum restraint. The targeting of civilian and economic infrastructure is unacceptable under any circumstances.

statements.speaker: Saudi FM Prince Faisal bin Farhan
statements.text: Saudi Arabia's patience with Iranian attacks is not unlimited. The Kingdom will take all necessary measures to protect Saudi territory and our people.

summary: UAE targeted by Iran since Day 3 — heaviest Gulf civilian casualties outside Israel. Day 27 (March 26): 2 people killed in Abu Dhabi by falling debris from intercepted ballistic missile (Pakistani and Indian nationals, Sweihan Street). UAE total: 11 killed, 163 injured, 1,800+ Iranian missiles and drones intercepted. UAE intercepted 15 ballistic missiles and 11 drones on Day 27 alone. GCC Secretary-General al-Budaiwi: Iranian attacks 'overcome all red lines.' UAE moving toward formal war entry discussions with US.

actions.text: UAE Patriot/THAAD systems intercept 241+ Iranian drones and 21 missiles targeting UAE territory. UAE closes Dubai International Airport for 6 hours during initial barrages.
UAE condemns Iranian attacks on civilian infrastructure and calls for restoration of Hormuz navigation. Iran attacks Fujairah oil hub for second consecutive day.

UAE in discussions on formal war entry including potential troop deployment, per WSJ/Bloomberg. UAE is reportedly discouraging US from agreeing to ceasefire before Iran's military capabilities are more thoroughly destroyed.

Two people killed in Abu Dhabi by falling debris from Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by UAE air defenses — first civilian deaths in UAE's capital. UAE discussing troop deployment alongside US forces and encouraging the US not to agree to a ceasefire before Iran's military capabilities are further degraded. UAE Foreign Policy Adviser Anwar Gargash: ceasefire must deliver 'long-term solution for Gulf security.' UAE cracking down on Iranian-owned assets in Dubai.

statements.speaker: UAE Ministry of Foreign Affairs
statements.text: The UAE condemns Iran's deliberate targeting of civilian airports and infrastructure. These attacks violate international law and will not go unanswered.

statements.speaker: UAE National Security Council (via WSJ)
statements.text: The UAE is actively discussing formal coalition entry and troop deployment. The UAE position: do not accept a ceasefire until Iran's offensive military capabilities are decisively degraded.

summary: Pakistan is the world's leading diplomatic mediator in the war. Islamabad quadrilateral summit (Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt) concluded March 29 — all 4 nations aligned on hosting US-Iran direct talks 'within days.' FM Dar: 'Pakistan is very honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides.' Trump reposted Dar's Hormuz deal announcement on Truth Social. Rubio-Araghchi meeting possible within 48-72 hours. 2 Pakistani ships already transited Hormuz under the deal.

actions.text: Pakistan offers to host US-Iran direct talks in Islamabad; delivers US 15-point framework to Iranian officials; PM Sharif holds calls with Trump and Pezeshkian.
Pakistan confirms delivery of Iranian 5-point counterproposal to US; German FM Wadephul says US-Iran direct meeting in Pakistan being "prepared for very soon."

Pakistan Deputy PM Dar announces Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships to transit Hormuz (2/day). Saudi, Turkey, Egypt FMs arrive Islamabad for emergency peace summit. Pezeshkian calls PM Sharif: "trust is needed" for talks to succeed.

Islamabad summit Day 1: Saudi FM Prince Faisal, Turkish FM Fidan, Egyptian FM Abdelatty physically arrive in Islamabad. FM Ishaq Dar confirms Iran agreed to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships/day through Hormuz. Germany FM Wadephul says direct US-Iran meeting in Islamabad "very soon." China Wang Yi praises Pakistan mediation.

First two Pakistani-flagged ships (MV Multan and P-Akili crude tanker carrying 80M+ litres) confirmed transiting Hormuz under Iran-Pakistan deal. Ships en route to Karachi, expected to dock March 31. Islamabad hosts quadrilateral summit with Saudi, Turkish, and Egyptian FMs — day 1 of 2-day emergency peace negotiations.

statements.speaker: Ishaq Dar, Deputy Prime Minister
statements.text: Pakistan has secured an agreement from Iran to allow 20 Pakistani-flagged ships to transit the Strait of Hormuz. Pakistan stands ready to host direct US-Iran talks in Islamabad.

statements.speaker: PM Shehbaz Sharif
statements.text: We had a productive 1-hour call with President Pezeshkian. Pakistan calls for an end to all attacks and hostilities. We are ready to host direct US-Iran talks as a neutral venue. Pakistan stands for peace.

statements.speaker: FM Ishaq Dar
statements.text: Pakistan is very honored to host and facilitate meaningful talks between the two sides in coming days. All four nations — Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, and Egypt — express full support for US-Iran direct talks in Islamabad.
